title: senna artemisioides subsp. alicia
intro: Senna artemisioides subsp. alicia is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, commonly known as the desert broom or senna, native to Australia. The medicinal properties of Senna artemisioides subsp. alicia lie in its ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of this plant include its use as a natural laxative, as well as its potential to help manage symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and other gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, Senna artemisioides subsp. alicia is often used in combination with other herbs, such as peppermint and ginger, to create teas, capsules, and tinctures that promote digestive health.
